question: Define a base class `MediaItem` with a virtual function `play()`. Derive `AudioFile` and `VideoFile` classes from `MediaItem`. Override `play()` in each derived class to simulate playing an audio or video file (e.g., printing 'Playing audio: [title]' or 'Playing video: [filename]'). Use an array of `MediaItem` pointers to demonstrate playing various media types polymorphically.

test_cases.expected:
===== Demonstrating Polymorphism =====
--- Audio Playback Initiated ---
Playing audio: Epic Soundtrack
Enjoy your music!
--------------------------------
--- Video Playback Initiated ---
Playing video: cpp_tutorial_p1.mp4 (Title: Tutorial Series Part 1)
Full screen recommended!
--------------------------------
--- Audio Playback Initiated ---
Playing audio: Morning Chill Mix
Enjoy your music!
--------------------------------
--- Video Playback Initiated ---
Playing video: japan_vlog.mov (Title: Travel Vlog - Japan)
Full screen recommended!
--------------------------------
======================================

errors: Uses C-style array and manual size tracking (`media_arr`, `media_count`) instead of `std::vector<std::unique_ptr<MediaItem>>` for polymorphic collection, which is less idiomatic, flexible, and error-prone for memory management.
Missing `override` keyword for virtual function overrides in derived classes, which reduces readability and prevents compiler from catching potential errors in function signatures.
The `play()` implementations in derived classes include slightly verbose or unnecessary print statements (e.g., 'Enjoy your music!', 'Full screen recommended!').

String concatenation in `VideoFile::play()` uses an unnecessary intermediate `std::string` variable and multiple `+=` operations, which can be less efficient and less readable than direct stream insertion for simple cases.
